english: ings; záb-lam (zab lam) the profound method or way, t́ábs-lam (thabs lam) method of the (proper) means (nif.) Mil.; bla-med-rdó-rjei lam (bla med rdo rjei lam), col. sṅágs-kyi lam (sngags kyi lam) denotes the Uma-doctrine or mysticism, v. dbú-ma (dbu ma); skyés-bu čúṅ-bai (skyes bu chung bai), briṅ-poi ('bring poi), and čén-poi lam (chen poi lam) three ways: that of a natural (sinful) man, that of the more advanced believer (but not: 'the happy mean' Cs.) and that of the saint, or the walk and conversation of the righteous, so also in draṅ-sroṅ-gi lam (drang srong gi lam) the saint's or hermit's course of life; dgé-ba bčúi lás-kyi lam spyód-pa (dge ba bcu'i las kyi lam spyod pa) to walk the way of practising the ten virtues Dzl. Comp. and deriv. lám-ka (lam ka) prob. = lam (lam), lám-ka-na (lam ka na) (another reading lám-kar (lam kar)), by the road-side Dzl. — lam-mḱan (lam mkhan) one well acquainted with the road, a guide Pth., also fig. — lam-gól (lam gol) by-way, secret path Sch. — lam-grógs (lam grogs) fellow-traveller, travelling companion. — lam-rgyúd (lam rgyud) = lam (lam) 3? lam-rgyúd lṅa (lam rgyud lnga) Dzl. 25, 18, the five classes of beings, cf. gró-ba (gro ba) II. — lam-rgyús-pa (lam rgyus pa) = lám-mḱan (lam mkhan). — lam-čén (lam chen) Schr. = rgya-lám (rgya lam). — lam-rtágs (lam rtags) the signs of the way being nearly accomplished i.e. the acquirements and perfections of a saint Mil. — lam-ltar-snaṅ (lam ltar snang) something looking like a road, but a spurious, wrong way Sch. — lam-stégs (lam stegs) seat, resting-place by the way-side; also fig. Glr. — lam-mdó (lam mdo) v. mdo (mdo). — lam-°drén-pa (lam 'dren pa), lam-sná-pa (lam sna pa) guide. — lám-pa (lam pa) 1. police-officer stationed on high-roads for seizing thieves or fugitives; toll-gatherer. 2. traveller, wayfarer Cs. 3. bell-wether W. — lám-po (lam po) = lam (lam), lam-po-čé (lam po che) 1. highway Sb.; also as a place for practising magic, nif. 2. way to heaven, = *t́ar-lam* (*thar lam*) W.(?) — lám-yig (lam yig) v. yi-ge (yi ge) extr. — lam-lóg (lam log) erroneous Mil. — lam-sraṅ (lam srang) lane, street.

1. but, yet, still, however Mil., Thgy., Glr.; lár-ni (lar ni) and lár-na (lar na) id.; occurs scarcely any more in col. language. — 2. *lar* (*lar*) (or *la-re* (*la re*)) *me* (*me*) C. none at all (?).

I. sbst., col. lás-ka (las ka), resp. pyag-lás (phyag las) W. *t́iṅ-le* (*thing le*). 1. action, act, deed, work, byi-dór-gyi las (byi dor gyi las) the act of sweeping Lex.; las-bzáṅ (las bzang), las-dkár (las dkar) a good work, virtuous action, las-ṅán (las ngan), las-nág (las nag) a bad, a wicked action, frq.; lus (lus) daṅ (dang) ṅag (ngag) daṅ (dang) yíd-kyi las (yid kyi las) actions, words, thoughts Dzl.; lás-kyi rnam-smíṅ (las kyi rnam smin) retribution, reward or punishment for human actions, frq. (cf. las-rgyu-°bras (las rgyu 'bras) below); lás-kyi mé-loṅ (las kyi me long) mirror of fate, mirror foreshadowing future events Glr.; lás-kyi búm-pa (las kyi bum pa) a certain vessel used in religious ceremonies Schl. 248; las (las) mazád pas (mazad pas) because the measure of his deeds was not yet full, his destiny was not yet fulfilled Dzl.; also destination in a general sense Was. (282); lás-kyi lhágy-ma (las kyi lhagy ma) lús-pa des (lus pa des) in consequence of the yet remaining rest of (unrequited) works Stg.; sṅón-las (sngon las) former action; las dbaṅ-bčós-su méd-pa (las dbang bcos su med pa) Pth. an accident which cannot be prevented; performance, transaction, business, las t́ams-čáu nus-pa (las thams cau nus pa) one who can do or perform every thing Do.; also the functions of some organ of the body Lt.; work; labour, manual labour, *lé-ka t́ób-pa* (*le ka thob pa*) to get work; las byéd-pa (las byed pa) B., C., *lé-ka čó-če* (*le ka co ce*), *táṅ-če* (*tang ce*) W. to do or perform a work, to work, also of things: to operate, to produce effects Wdn; mḱar-las-byed-mí (mkhar las byed mi) workmen employed in building Mil.; dúr-las byéd-pa (dur las byed pa) to attend to the graves, i.e. to perform the sepulchral rites and ceremonies; zaṅ-zíṅ-las byéd-pa (zang zing las byed pa) to carry on business, to trade, to traffic Mil.; lás-su (las su) as a task, according to one's occupation, trade, or business, by virtue of one's office, ex officio (nif.) Mil.; lás-su rúṅ-bar (las su rung bar) duly, rightly, perfectly, comme il faut Mil.; lás-su byá-ba (las su bya ba) v. below (extr.). — 2. sometimes: secular business, *lé-ke ṅáṅ-na* (*le ke ngang na*) in business-affairs, in practical life. — 3. effect of actions, and in a special sense: merit, las zád-pa (las zad pa) the merits being over, having an end Thgy. (cf. 1, above). — 4. the doctrine of works and their consequences, of retribution, las mi bdén (las mi bden) that doctrine is not true Thgy. Comp. and deriv. lás-ka (las ka) 1. col. work, labour, v. above. 2. Sch. and Wts.: dignity, rank, title. — las-skál (las skal) retributive fate, = las-pró (las pro). — lás-mḱan (las mkhan) workman Cs. — las-rgyu-°brás (las rgyu 'bras) either for: las (las) daṅ (dang) rgyu-°bras (rgyu 'bras) works and their fruits (which in Thgy. are divided into bsód-nams-ma-yín-pai las-rgyu-°brás (bsod nams ma yin pai las rgyu 'bras) sinful deeds, bsód-nams-kyi las-rgyu-°bras (bsod nams kyi las rgyu 'bras)
